patterns: Extract JWT generation into a standalone helper function (_build_jwt) for reusability and testability
Use early return pattern for validation — each error returns immediately, keeping happy path at minimal indentation
Defensive request body validation — check for None and isinstance(dict) before accessing fields
Use Flask jsonify() for consistent JSON responses with automatic Content-Type headers
Inline auth pattern: call get_current_user() + get_user_permissions() directly in route, avoid decorator wrapper ordering bugs
Iterative BFS cycle detection with collections.deque + db.session.expire_all() to avoid stale identity-map cache
Use db.session.get(Model, id) instead of deprecated Model.query.get() for SQLAlchemy 2.0 compatibility
ORM relationship operations (user.roles.append/remove) for many-to-many with idempotency checks
Use middleware.check_permission decorator directly for simple permission checks (single decorator, no nesting)
Extract document serialization into standalone helper function for consistent response format
Partial update pattern: check each field independently with 'updated' flag for conditional commit
Combine tenant_id filtering into query (filter_by with tenant_id) for implicit cross-tenant 404
architecturalNotes: ATU-001: Login endpoint uses early return + extracted JWT helper. All auth responses use standard {status, data/message} format.
ATU-002: Role management uses inline auth (no decorators), BFS cycle detection, ORM relationship ops. 5 endpoints all scoped by tenant_id.
ATU-003: Document CRUD uses check_permission decorator correctly (single layer), extracted serialize helper, partial PUT updates. 5 endpoints with owner checks.

atus.title: Auth Routes - Login Endpoint
atus.description: Implement POST /login in routes_auth.py: validate credentials, generate JWT with user_id+tenant_id+exp, return token

retainedTraits: Extract JWT generation into a standalone helper function (_build_jwt) for reusability and testability
Use early return pattern for validation — each error returns immediately, keeping happy path at minimal indentation
Defensive request body validation — check for None and isinstance(dict) before accessing fields
Use Flask jsonify() for consistent JSON responses with automatic Content-Type headers

atus.title: Role Management Routes
atus.description: Implement all role management endpoints in routes_role.py: create/list roles, update permissions, assign/remove user roles, cycle detection

samples.name: sample1
samples.failureReason: _require_role_manage wrapper accessed g.current_user before check_permission set it

retainedTraits: Inline auth pattern: call get_current_user() + get_user_permissions() directly in route, avoid decorator wrapper ordering bugs
Iterative BFS cycle detection with collections.deque + db.session.expire_all() to avoid stale identity-map cache
Use db.session.get(Model, id) instead of deprecated Model.query.get() for SQLAlchemy 2.0 compatibility
ORM relationship operations (user.roles.append/remove) for many-to-many with idempotency checks
All queries scoped by tenant_id — cross-tenant ops return 404 (no information leakage)

atus.title: Document CRUD Routes + Full Integration
atus.description: Implement all document endpoints in routes_document.py: list/get/create/update/delete with RBAC, tenant isolation, owner checks

retainedTraits: Use middleware.check_permission decorator directly for simple permission checks (single decorator, no nesting)
Extract document serialization into standalone helper function for consistent response format
Partial update pattern: check each field independently with 'updated' flag for conditional commit
Combine tenant_id filtering into query (filter_by with tenant_id) for implicit cross-tenant 404
